primary_diagnosis.name: Severe prolonged Raynaud's phenomenon attack (secondary), on a background of limited cutaneous systemic sclerosis (CREST variant)

pathophysiology_summary: Raynaud's phenomenon is exaggerated cold- and stress-induced digital arteriolar vasospasm producing the classic triphasic colour change (pallor from vasospasm, cyanosis from deoxygenation, then reactive hyperaemia on reperfusion). This attack is SECONDARY Raynaud's arising from limited cutaneous systemic sclerosis (the CREST constellation: Calcinosis, Raynaud's, oEsophageal dysmotility/reflux, Sclerodactyly, Telangiectasia; anti-centromere antibody positive). Unlike primary Raynaud's, secondary Raynaud's in systemic sclerosis is accompanied by structural microvascular obliteration, so attacks can be more severe and prolonged and carry a materially higher risk of critical digital ischaemia, digital ulceration and tissue loss. Tonight's attack is unusually severe and prolonged (~45 minutes, only partially reperfusing indoors), which raises the question the triage must answer: is this still reversible vasospasm, or evolving fixed/critical ischaemia? The reassuring in-person findings (bilateral symmetry, strong bounding radial and ulnar pulses, intact palmar arches on Allen's test, delayed but recovering capillary refill, no gangrene or ulcer) indicate severe reversible vasospasm rather than macrovascular arterial occlusion — but those findings require a clinician's hands and cannot be obtained over telehealth.

differential.evidence_basis: Cold-triggered stark-white then blue fingertips with severe pain in a patient with a prior Raynaud's label.

differential.diagnosis: Acute arterial embolism / thromboembolic digital ischaemia
differential.evidence_basis: Acute severe painful pale cold digits could represent a limb/digit-threatening arterial occlusion — must be actively excluded, and telehealth cannot palpate pulses to do so.

differential.diagnosis: Critical digital ischaemia with impending tissue loss
differential.evidence_basis: Prolonged severe ischaemic pain raises the possibility of ischaemia progressing beyond reversible vasospasm.
Small-vessel digital ischaemia — but classically fixed rather than transient and strongly associated with smoking.

differential_progression.scoring_note: At the opening, the dangerous mimics (arterial occlusion, critical ischaemia) must sit alongside Raynaud's; the AI should NOT prematurely close on 'just Raynaud's' nor leap to calling an ambulance before taking a focused history.

differential.evidence_basis: Bilateral symmetrical cold-induced triphasic colour change, established Raynaud's pattern, now partially reperfusing — typical of severe vasospasm.

differential.diagnosis: Acute arterial embolism / thromboembolic digital ischaemia
differential.evidence_basis: Bilateral symmetry and cold-triggering with partial recovery make an embolic occlusion less likely, but it cannot be excluded without examining pulses.
Lifelong non-smoker with transient cold-induced rather than fixed ischaemia.

differential_progression.scoring_note: History shifts Raynaud's to clearly leading and downgrades Buerger's, but arterial occlusion remains a 'cannot-exclude-remotely' item that justifies in-person assessment.

differential.diagnosis: Severe secondary Raynaud's attack, on a background of systemic sclerosis
differential.evidence_basis: No black digit, no ulcer, tips already reddening — reversible vasospasm; secondary-cause clues (sclerodactyly, reflux) point to underlying connective-tissue disease.
Kept on the list as the specific thing the time-bound safety-net guards against (fixed pallor/cyanosis >2h, or a blackening digit).

differential_progression.scoring_note: The negative screen for gangrene/ulcer is reassuring but does NOT let the AI claim to have excluded critical ischaemia remotely — the correct posture is conservative urgent in-person assessment with a time-bound escalation threshold.

differential.diagnosis: Severe prolonged secondary Raynaud's phenomenon attack in limited cutaneous systemic sclerosis
differential.evidence_basis: Reversible vasospasm confirmed in-person (intact pulses, recovering capillary refill, no tissue loss); underlying systemic sclerosis to be confirmed on outpatient work-up.

differential_progression.scoring_note: The acute problem is a severe reversible vasospastic attack; the enduring finding is a newly-clued underlying systemic sclerosis needing referral — both must be captured.

red_flags.description: Unusually severe and PROLONGED ischaemic attack (~45 min) only partially reperfusing despite rewarming
red_flags.clinical_significance: Duration and severity beyond the patient's usual pattern raise the possibility of ischaemia progressing beyond reversible vasospasm; in secondary Raynaud's the digital circulation is already structurally compromised.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Failure to arrange timely assessment could allow evolving critical ischaemia to progress to digital ulceration or tissue loss.

red_flags.description: A digit turning black (digital necrosis/gangrene) or fixed unrecovering pallor/cyanosis
red_flags.clinical_significance: A blackening or fixed pulseless white/blue digit signals established critical ischaemia — a limb/digit emergency.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Missed critical ischaemia leads to irreversible tissue loss / amputation.

red_flags.description: Cannot exclude acute arterial occlusion / embolism via telehealth (no ability to palpate radial/ulnar pulses or perform Allen's test)
red_flags.clinical_significance: The single most dangerous mimic requires hands-on vascular examination the AI Doctor cannot perform; this drives the need for in-person assessment.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Falsely reassuring the patient and missing a macrovascular occlusion threatening the digits.

red_flags.description: Digital ulcer developing signs of infection (redness, swelling, purulent discharge)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Infected digital ulcers are a recognised complication of secondary Raynaud's/systemic sclerosis and need urgent care.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Progression to deep infection or osteomyelitis of the digit.

red_flags.description: Features of an underlying connective-tissue disease (sclerodactyly/skin tightening, reflux, prior positive autoantibody) indicating SECONDARY Raynaud's
red_flags.clinical_significance: Secondary Raynaud's carries a substantially higher risk of digital ulceration and critical ischaemia than primary Raynaud's and mandates specialist work-up.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Under-appreciating the risk and failing to refer for diagnosis and preventive management.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Anchoring on the dramatic stark-white painful presentation and calling 000 for an ambulance, when bilateral symmetry and partial reperfusion point to a resolving vasospasm rather than a life-threatening event.

Dismissing a first severe, prolonged attack as trivial Raynaud's and giving only self-care advice, missing the possibility of evolving critical digital ischaemia and the need for in-person assessment.
Claiming to have excluded acute arterial occlusion or critical ischaemia without the ability to palpate pulses, perform Allen's test, or objectively assess capillary refill.
Treating the attack in isolation and overlooking the sclerodactyly, reflux and prior abnormal autoimmune blood test that point to underlying systemic sclerosis requiring referral.
Guaranteeing the pain will completely resolve within 15 minutes of rewarming — an over-certain reassurance that could delay escalation if it does not.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Allen's test / patency of the palmar arches
Clinician skin examination for sclerodactyly, calcinosis, telangiectasia and digital ulcers
Blood pressure, heart rate, temperature and SpO2 (no home device offered)
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Instruct immediate whole-body rewarming (layers, warm — not hot — water over hands/wrists, hot drink) and arrange urgent in-person assessment tonight (after-hours GP / urgent care) because telehealth cannot exclude critical digital ischaemia or arterial occlusion. Escalate to the emergency department if any fingertip stays completely white/blue and numb for more than two hours despite core rewarming, or if a finger turns black.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Moderate. The vasospastic mechanism is likely, but the AI cannot confirm reperfusion or exclude the dangerous mimics remotely; the real risks are over-triage (000 for a resolving attack) and under-triage (self-care only, missing evolving critical ischaemia).

guidelines_referenced.reference: Therapeutic Guidelines (Rheumatology) — Raynaud phenomenon and systemic sclerosis (Australian)
guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: Distinguish primary from secondary Raynaud's; secondary Raynaud's warrants investigation for connective-tissue disease and carries higher digital-ischaemia risk; dihydropyridine calcium-channel blockers (e.g. nifedipine) are first-line preventive therapy.
